name: Milan (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the silver field of the city's arms
the cross of Saint Ambrose, patron of Milan

description: A white field bearing a red Greek cross (the cross of Saint Ambrose) extending to the edges.
history: The red cross on white is the cross of Saint Ambrose, patron saint of Milan, and has been the city's emblem since the medieval period. The Crusader state of the Lombard League adopted the same emblem.

features.symbolism: the cross of Saint Ambrose, patron saint of Milan; in continuous civic use since the medieval period
